content.text:
All verifications are complete. Let me summarize my findings:

**Implementation verification:**

1. **`Controllers/FilesController.cs`** — `"application/octet-stream"` extracted to `private const string DefaultContentType = "application/octet-stream";` with a doc comment. It is referenced both in the Download endpoint's XML doc comment (`<see cref="DefaultContentType"/>`) and in the ternary expression (`: DefaultContentType;`). ✓

2. **`Configuration/FileServiceOptions.cs`** — The `"FileService"` section name was placed on the options class as `public const string SectionName = "FileService";` with a doc comment (the preferred location per the task). ✓

3. **`Program.cs`** — References the constant via `TestProject.Configuration.FileServiceOptions.SectionName` instead of the raw literal. A separate local const in Program.cs was correctly avoided (which would have been duplicative). ✓

**Behavior preserved:** The extractions are pure refactor — no logic changes. The test suite (`ProgramWiringTests.ResolveSectionName`) explicitly handles both literal and constant-reference forms and asserts the section name resolves to `"FileService"`, so the contract is pinned.

**Build:** 0 warnings, 0 errors.
**Tests:** 398 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped.

No quality issues found. The implementation is minimal, follows existing conventions (constants co-located with their conceptual owner, XML doc comments match the project style), contains no dead code, and did not expand scope.

(1) The \"application/octet-stream\" literal in FilesController.cs was extracted to a private `const string DefaultContentType` with a doc comment, referenced both in the Download XML doc comment (`<see cref=\"DefaultContentType\"/>`) and the ternary expression. (2) The \"FileService\" section name was placed on the options class as `public const string SectionName` (the preferred location per the task description) and is referenced from Program.cs via `FileServiceOptions.SectionName`; a redundant local const in Program.cs was correctly avoided. Build: 0 warnings, 0 errors. Tests: 398 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. The ProgramWiringTests/ResolveSectionName helper explicitly accommodates both the literal and constant-reference forms and pins the resolved value to \"FileService\", so the refactor is provably behavior-preserving. No dead code, no scope expansion, no invented patterns — constants are co-located with their conceptual owners and follow the project's existing XML doc-comment style.","issues":[]}
